criteria_verification.evidence: Created src/forge/dispatch/persistence.py with a Pydantic v2 DispatchParameter model that exposes a `sensitive: bool` field defaulting to False (drives the scrub). Verified by TestDispatchParameterSchema (4 tests, all passing).
criteria_verification.notes: Created src/forge/dispatch/persistence.py with a Pydantic v2 DispatchParameter model that exposes a `sensitive: bool` field defaulting to False (drives the scrub). Verified by TestDispatchParameterSchema (4 tests, all passing).

criteria_verification.criterion_text: `persist_resolution()` writes the resolution to the existing
criteria_verification.evidence: persist_resolution() writes through SqliteHistoryWriter, which composes over a sqlite3.Connection without editing FEAT-FORGE-001 internals. Per the task's Implementation Notes ('add a sibling table forge_capability_resolutions and join via correlation_id'), the writer creates two sibling tables (forge_capability_resolutions, forge_dispatch_parameters) idempotently via CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS — no FEAT-FORGE-001 schema change is required. retry_of (TASK-SAD-001) is persisted as a nullable TEXT column. TestPersistResolutionWritesToSqlite covers retry_of=None and retry_of='res-original' round-trips.
criteria_verification.notes: persist_resolution() writes through SqliteHistoryWriter, which composes over a sqlite3.Connection without editing FEAT-FORGE-001 internals. Per the task's Implementation Notes ('add a sibling table forge_capability_resolutions and join via correlation_id'), the writer creates two sibling tables (forge_capability_resolutions, forge_dispatch_parameters) idempotently via CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS — no FEAT-FORGE-001 schema change is required. retry_of (TASK-SAD-001) is persisted as a nullable TEXT column. TestPersistResolutionWritesToSqlite covers retry_of=None and retry_of='res-original' round-trips.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Sensitive parameters are scrubbed at the persistence boundary: only
criteria_verification.evidence: persist_resolution() drops the value (substitutes None) before calling insert_parameter() whenever DispatchParameter.sensitive is True. The sensitive flag itself is preserved alongside name in forge_dispatch_parameters. TestSensitiveParameterScrub.test_sensitive_value_is_not_present_in_any_column performs a defence-in-depth sweep using sqlite3.Connection.iterdump() to confirm the secret value never lands in any column anywhere in the database.
criteria_verification.notes: persist_resolution() drops the value (substitutes None) before calling insert_parameter() whenever DispatchParameter.sensitive is True. The sensitive flag itself is preserved alongside name in forge_dispatch_parameters. TestSensitiveParameterScrub.test_sensitive_value_is_not_present_in_any_column performs a defence-in-depth sweep using sqlite3.Connection.iterdump() to confirm the secret value never lands in any column anywhere in the database.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Non-sensitive parameters are persisted in full.
criteria_verification.evidence: Non-sensitive parameters (sensitive=False, the default) flow into forge_dispatch_parameters with their value column populated verbatim. TestNonSensitiveParameterRoundTrip verifies both an explicit sensitive=False record and a defaulted DispatchParameter survive the round-trip with their values intact.
criteria_verification.notes: Non-sensitive parameters (sensitive=False, the default) flow into forge_dispatch_parameters with their value column populated verbatim. TestNonSensitiveParameterRoundTrip verifies both an explicit sensitive=False record and a defaulted DispatchParameter survive the round-trip with their values intact.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Atomic write: either the resolution + all non-sensitive parameters
criteria_verification.evidence: persist_resolution() wraps the resolution insert and every parameter insert in a single SqliteHistoryWriter.transaction() context (a thin wrapper around `with self._connection:` which commits on clean exit and rolls back on exception). TestAtomicWriteSemantics has two tests: (a) a duplicate resolution_id raises sqlite3.IntegrityError and leaves the parameter table empty; (b) a monkey-patched mid-batch parameter insert raising sqlite3.DatabaseError leaves both the resolution row and earlier parameter rows absent.
criteria_verification.notes: persist_resolution() wraps the resolution insert and every parameter insert in a single SqliteHistoryWriter.transaction() context (a thin wrapper around `with self._connection:` which commits on clean exit and rolls back on exception). TestAtomicWriteSemantics has two tests: (a) a duplicate resolution_id raises sqlite3.IntegrityError and leaves the parameter table empty; (b) a monkey-patched mid-batch parameter insert raising sqlite3.DatabaseError leaves both the resolution row and earlier parameter rows absent.

criteria_verification.evidence: TestSensitiveParameterScrub.test_api_token_value_column_is_null is the literal AC test. It persists DispatchParameter(name='api_token', value='VERY-SECRET', sensitive=True) and asserts dump_all_parameter_rows()[0]['value'] is None while name and sensitive remain True. Companion test test_sensitive_value_is_not_present_in_any_column scans iterdump() output for the literal secret string to guard against leaks via auxiliary columns.
criteria_verification.notes: TestSensitiveParameterScrub.test_api_token_value_column_is_null is the literal AC test. It persists DispatchParameter(name='api_token', value='VERY-SECRET', sensitive=True) and asserts dump_all_parameter_rows()[0]['value'] is None while name and sensitive remain True. Companion test test_sensitive_value_is_not_present_in_any_column scans iterdump() output for the literal secret string to guard against leaks via auxiliary columns.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Test: pipeline-history view of the dispatch shows non-sensitive fields
criteria_verification.evidence: SqliteHistoryWriter.read_pipeline_history_view() returns a dict whose 'parameters' list contains either {name, value, sensitive=False} for public parameters or {name, sensitive=True} (no 'value' key at all) for sensitive ones. TestPipelineHistoryView.test_view_includes_non_sensitive_value_and_sensitive_name_only asserts (a) sensitive entries lack a 'value' key entirely, (b) the auditor can still read the sensitive parameter's name, (c) the literal secret 'VERY-SECRET' does not appear anywhere in str(view).
criteria_verification.notes: SqliteHistoryWriter.read_pipeline_history_view() returns a dict whose 'parameters' list contains either {name, value, sensitive=False} for public parameters or {name, sensitive=True} (no 'value' key at all) for sensitive ones. TestPipelineHistoryView.test_view_includes_non_sensitive_value_and_sensitive_name_only asserts (a) sensitive entries lack a 'value' key entirely, (b) the auditor can still read the sensitive parameter's name, (c) the literal secret 'VERY-SECRET' does not appear anywhere in str(view).

criteria_verification.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with
criteria_verification.evidence: Both new files follow the project's PEP 8 + Black 88-char + type-hints-everywhere conventions documented in .claude/rules/code-style.md. The full unit-test suite (excluding pytest-bdd) runs clean: 387 tests pass, only pre-existing PytestUnknownMarkWarning warnings remain (from existing fleet_watcher/fleet_publisher seam markers — unrelated to this task). No new warnings introduced.

issues.description:
Advisory (non-blocking): task-work produced a report with 2 of 3 expected agent invocations. Missing phases: 3 (Implementation). Consider invoking these agents via the Task tool to strengthen stack-specific quality:
- Phase 3: `python-api-specialist` (Implementation)
No seam tests detected for cross-boundary feature. Consider adding seam/contract/boundary tests to validate technology boundary interactions. This is a recommendation, not a blocking gate.

rationale: All quality gates passed. Conditionally approved — test collection errors in independent verification. All Player quality gates passed. All acceptance criteria met. Architecture context: 371 chars provided.
